steps.title: Apply the triangle inequality for the lower bound
steps.content:
The third side must be longer than the difference of the two known sides: $$|12-6| < x$$.
Since $$|12-6| = 6$$, we get $$6 < x$$.

steps.title: Apply the triangle inequality for the upper bound and combine
steps.content:
The third side must be shorter than the sum of the two known sides: $$x < 12+6$$.
Thus $$x < 18$$.
Combining both inequalities gives $$6 < x < 18$$.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
